item.title: Where did 400 MiB go?
item.description:
While doing some routine upgrades today, I restarted all 60+ pods of a Node.js websocket app. Every pod came back sitting at ~330 MiB of memory, but one of them, after a few minutes, spiked from ~330 MiB to 500, then to 640 and stayed there.

This wasn’t new to me. I’ve been staring at this same pattern for a month and a half, but never got the chance to actually investigate it. V8’s garbage collection was my first bet, but since this is an important workload for the company, I didn’t want to commit to any changes before confirming the root cause.
